name: Chicago Cook Workforce Partnership
notes: Main coordinating body for workforce development in Chicago and Cook County. Operates a network of American Job Centers across the region.

name: American Job Center — Loop (WorkNet Chicago)
notes: Federally funded one-stop center. Free services for job seekers including skills assessments, job listings, and referrals to training programs.

name: American Job Center — South Side (Kennedy-King College)
notes: Located at Kennedy-King College. Serves Englewood, Auburn Gresham, and surrounding South Side communities.

name: Cara Collective
notes: Serves individuals facing significant employment barriers including homelessness, incarceration history, and poverty. Paid transitional employment with coaching.

name: North Lawndale Employment Network (NLEN)
notes: Specializes in workforce development for returning citizens and residents of North and South Lawndale. Operates Sweet Beginnings beekeeping social enterprise.

name: Chicago Urban League Workforce Development
notes: Provides full-service employment placement and digital empowerment workshops. Hosts the annual Citywide Job Fair.

name: City Colleges Workforce Empowerment Initiative (WEI)
notes: Tuition-free short-term certificate programs at City Colleges for unemployed/underemployed Chicago residents. Programs include stipends, books, and transportation cards.

name: Greencorps Chicago
notes: Paid green-industry employment program with on-the-job training. Focuses on returning residents. Run by Chicago DCASE.

name: YWCA Metropolitan Chicago — Workforce Development
notes: Workforce programs focused on women and survivors of domestic violence. Includes career coaching and training referrals.

name: CEDA of Cook County — LIHEAP Energy Assistance
notes: Primary LIHEAP administrator for Cook County. Apply Oct 1–Aug 15, 2026. Provides one-time bill payments directly to utilities plus reconnection assistance up to $1,500.

name: ComEd Low-Income Discount (LID) Program
notes: New Jan 2026 program. Sliding-scale monthly electric discount for incomes up to 300% FPL. Households approved for LIHEAP are automatically enrolled. Apply online at ComEd.com/SAM.

name: Nicor Gas Low-Income Discount Program
notes: 5–84% monthly gas bill discount for incomes up to 300% FPL. Serving northern Illinois suburbs. Apply through your local Community Action Agency or Nicor directly.

name: Peoples Gas Low-Income Discount Program
notes: 5–84% monthly gas bill discount for Chicago residents with incomes up to 300% FPL. Customers already enrolled in LIHEAP are automatically qualified.

name: Illinois LIHEAP — Online Application
notes: State-run heating and energy assistance. Apply online at helpillinoisfamilies.com. Income must be at or below 60% of state median income (up to ~$76,884/yr for family of 4). No immigration status required.

name: City of Chicago Utility Billing Relief (UBR)
notes: Chicago residents can get a 50% reduction on City water, sewer rate, and water-sewer tax. Also offers complete debt forgiveness on City utility accounts for eligible households.

name: Chicago DFSS Rental Assistance Program (RAP)
address: Apply via 311 or nearest Community Service Center
notes: City of Chicago program for residents at risk of homelessness. Helps with rent if income was lost or emergency occurred. Funded by HUD Emergency Solutions Grant. Funding subject to availability.

name: Illinois Court-Based Rental Assistance (CBRAP)
notes: For tenants AND landlords with an active eviction case in court. No citizenship status required. Administered by Illinois Housing Development Authority (IHDA). Tenants do not need to show COVID hardship.

name: All Chicago — Homeless Prevention Fund
notes: State Homeless Prevention Fund administered by All Chicago. Call 311 and ask for short-term assistance. Helps stabilize housing for those at risk of homelessness.

name: Neighborhood Housing Services (NHS) Chicago
notes: Nonprofit providing affordable home resources, emergency assistance grants, and housing counseling. HUD-approved housing counseling agency.

name: Cook County Legal Aid for Housing and Debt (CCLAHD)
notes: Free legal advice and mediation for Cook County residents facing eviction or debt. Can help negotiate 'pay and stay' agreements or contest unlawful evictions.

name: NAMI Chicago
notes: National Alliance on Mental Illness local affiliate. Free mental health support, peer education, and helpline. Call 833-NAMI-CHI for guidance and referrals.

name: Thresholds
notes: Illinois' largest mental health nonprofit. Provides community-based mental health and substance use treatment. Many services are free or sliding scale.

name: Chicago Department of Public Health — Mental Health Centers
hours: Varies by location; call for nearest center
notes: City-operated mental health centers offering free and sliding-scale services. Call to find the nearest center to you.

name: Chicago Department of Public Health — Free Clinics
notes: Chicago runs several free and sliding-scale public health clinics. Call CDPH or visit their website to find the nearest location.

name: ACCESS Community Health Network
hours: Varies by site; call or visit website
notes: Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) with 35+ locations across Chicago. Sliding-scale fees based on income. Accepts Medicaid, Medicare, and uninsured patients.

name: Heartland Health Centers
notes: FQHC serving Albany Park, Rogers Park, and surrounding neighborhoods. Sliding-scale fees; no one turned away. Accepts most insurance and uninsured patients.

name: Erie Family Health Centers
notes: FQHC with multiple locations on Chicago's North, Northwest, and West Sides. Sliding-scale fees based on income. Accepts all patients regardless of insurance or immigration status.

name: Brown Elephant Resale (Howard Brown Health)
notes: Affordable resale shops benefiting Howard Brown Health's LGBTQ+ health programs. Locations in Andersonville, Rogers Park, and Oak Park.

name: Salvation Army Family Stores — Chicago
notes: Affordable thrift stores across Chicago. Salvation Army also provides emergency assistance with rent, utilities, and food at select corps community centers.

name: Free Store Chicagoland
notes: Completely free clothing and household goods. No income verification required. Serves Auburn Gresham and surrounding South Side neighborhoods.
